stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Engineer Norma Merrick Sklarek trained in modernist and international-style architecture in New York.
  • Sklarek worked for a large architecture firm that was contracted in 1965 to design buildings for a major California civic center.
  • Most of the civic center’s buildings exhibit modernist and international-style design.
  • At the time, Sklarek’s contributions were not publicly credited.
  • Based on the buildings’ design vocabulary, historians believe Sklarek led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings.
stem: The student wants to specify why historians believe Sklarek led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Although Sklarek wasn’t publicly credited at the time, historians believe she led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings.
Most of the civic center’s buildings, designed by a firm that employed Sklarek, exhibit modernist and international-style design.
Sklarek, an engineer who trained in modernist and international-style architecture in New York, is believed to have led the design of most of the civic center’s buildings.
Because most of the civic center’s buildings exhibit the modernist and international-style design vocabulary Sklarek had trained in, historians believe she led their design.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. It specifies the reason for historians’ belief: the buildings’ design vocabulary matches the style Sklarek trained in.

Choice A states the belief but not the reason. Choice B emphasizes the buildings’ styles without linking them to Sklarek’s authorship. Choice C identifies Sklarek and the belief but doesn’t specify the historians’ reasoning.
